comment_text.sn12.68:1.1: Musīla is mentioned only here. | Saviṭṭha may also appear at AN 3.21, but it may be a different person, as that monk’s name is also spelled Samiddha, and the time, place, and people are all different. | Nārada appears in AN 5.50. | This monastery was favored by Ānanda, who was the teacher of Ghosita, the monastery’s donor (SN 35.129). He stayed there following the Buddha’s passing and the First Council (SN 22.90:4.2). Many, perhaps all, of the discourses featuring Ānanda here were set in the period after the Buddha’s death, including this one.
comment_text.sn12.68:1.3: This is a standard set of grounds for belief, which play a limited role in supporting the search for truth, but do not yield any definitive answers (MN 95, MN 101, MN 102, SN 35.153, AN 3.65, AN 3.66, AN 4.193). | Parivitakka means “train of thought”, as when the Buddha reads Kāpaṭika’s thoughts (MN 95:12.6). Ākāra in this context means “reason”, as at MN 47:10.2. | Khanti is usually better rendered “acceptance” than the common “patience”.
comment_text.sn12.68:1.6: This emphasizes that dependent origination is directly known through experience.
comment_text.sn12.68:5.2: This is a particularly blunt expression for Nibbana.
comment_text.sn12.68:6.1: The question of who, exactly, qualified as perfected became critical in the Saṅgha when the Buddha was no longer present to decide such matters. It was the decisive issue leading to the first schism between the ancestral Sthavira and Mahāsaṅghika schools, which happened after Ashoka, perhaps two centuries after the Buddha.
comment_text.sn12.68:6.2: Silence is usually taken as assent, and the commentary says this is the case here: Musīla was in fact an arahant.
comment_text.sn12.68:13.2: Saviṭṭha’s error lay in assuming that seeing cessation implies arahantship. To “truly see” and similar terms signify stream-entry (or above), while the end of grasping signifies perfection.
comment_text.sn12.68:14.1: The question of who is an arahant has the potential to tear apart a community, as followers align with their preferred saint. Ānanda, aware of this, is swift to restore harmony.

root_text.sn12.68:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ āyasmā ca musilo āyasmā ca paviṭṭho āyasmā ca nārado āyasmā ca ānando kosambiyaṁ viharanti ghositārāme.
root_text.sn12.68:1.2: Atha kho āyasmā paviṭṭho āyasmantaṁ musilaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn12.68:1.3: “aññatreva, āvuso musila, saddhāya aññatra ruciyā aññatra anussavā aññatra ākāraparivitakkā aññatra diṭṭhinijjhānakkhantiyā atthāyasmato musilassa paccattameva ñāṇaṁ:
root_text.sn12.68:1.5: “Aññatreva, āvuso paviṭṭha, saddhāya aññatra ruciyā aññatra anussavā aññatra ākāraparivitakkā aññatra diṭṭhinijjhānakkhantiyā ahametaṁ jānāmi ahametaṁ passāmi:
root_text.sn12.68:2.1: “Aññatreva, āvuso musila, saddhāya aññatra ruciyā aññatra anussavā aññatra ākāraparivitakkā aññatra diṭṭhinijjhānakkhantiyā atthāyasmato musilassa paccattameva ñāṇaṁ:
root_text.sn12.68:2.12: “Aññatreva, āvuso paviṭṭha, saddhāya aññatra ruciyā aññatra anussavā aññatra ākāraparivitakkā aññatra diṭṭhinijjhānakkhantiyā ahametaṁ jānāmi ahametaṁ passāmi:
root_text.sn12.68:3.1: “Aññatreva, āvuso musila, saddhāya aññatra ruciyā aññatra anussavā aññatra ākāraparivitakkā aññatra diṭṭhinijjhānakkhantiyā atthāyasmato musilassa paccattameva ñāṇaṁ:
root_text.sn12.68:3.3: “Aññatreva, āvuso paviṭṭha, saddhāya aññatra ruciyā aññatra anussavā aññatra ākāraparivitakkā aññatra diṭṭhinijjhānakkhantiyā ahametaṁ jānāmi ahametaṁ passāmi:
root_text.sn12.68:4.1: “Aññatreva, āvuso musila, saddhāya aññatra ruciyā aññatra anussavā aññatra ākāraparivitakkā aññatra diṭṭhinijjhānakkhantiyā atthāyasmato musilassa paccattameva ñāṇaṁ:
root_text.sn12.68:4.12: “Aññatreva, āvuso paviṭṭha, saddhāya aññatra ruciyā aññatra anussavā aññatra ākāraparivitakkā aññatra diṭṭhinijjhānakkhantiyā ahametaṁ jānāmi ahametaṁ passāmi:
root_text.sn12.68:5.1: “Aññatreva, āvuso musila, saddhāya aññatra ruciyā aññatra anussavā aññatra ākāraparivitakkā aññatra diṭṭhinijjhānakkhantiyā atthāyasmato musilassa paccattameva ñāṇaṁ:
root_text.sn12.68:5.3: “Aññatreva, āvuso paviṭṭha, saddhāya aññatra ruciyā aññatra anussavā aññatra ākāraparivitakkā aññatra diṭṭhinijjhānakkhantiyā ahametaṁ jānāmi ahametaṁ passāmi:
root_text.sn12.68:6.2: Evaṁ vutte, āyasmā musilo tuṇhī ahosi.
root_text.sn12.68:7.1: Atha kho āyasmā nārado āyasmantaṁ paviṭṭhaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn12.68:7.7: Byākarotu ca me āyasmā nārado etaṁ pañhaṁ.
root_text.sn12.68:8.1: Aññatreva, āvuso nārada, saddhāya aññatra ruciyā aññatra anussavā aññatra ākāraparivitakkā aññatra diṭṭhinijjhānakkhantiyā atthāyasmato nāradassa paccattameva ñāṇaṁ:
root_text.sn12.68:8.3: “Aññatreva, āvuso paviṭṭha, saddhāya aññatra ruciyā aññatra anussavā aññatra ākāraparivitakkā aññatra diṭṭhinijjhānakkhantiyā ahametaṁ jānāmi ahametaṁ passāmi:
root_text.sn12.68:9.1: “Aññatreva, āvuso nārada, saddhāya aññatra ruciyā aññatra anussavā aññatra ākāraparivitakkā aññatra diṭṭhinijjhānakkhantiyā atthāyasmato nāradassa paccattameva ñāṇaṁ—
root_text.sn12.68:9.4: “Aññatreva, āvuso paviṭṭha, saddhāya aññatra ruciyā aññatra anussavā aññatra ākāraparivitakkā aññatra diṭṭhinijjhānakkhantiyā ahametaṁ jānāmi ahametaṁ passāmi:
root_text.sn12.68:10.1: “Aññatreva, āvuso nārada, saddhāya aññatra ruciyā aññatra anussavā aññatra ākāraparivitakkā aññatra diṭṭhinijjhānakkhantiyā atthāyasmato nāradassa paccattameva ñāṇaṁ:
root_text.sn12.68:10.3: “Aññatreva, āvuso paviṭṭha, saddhāya aññatra ruciyā aññatra anussavā aññatra ākāraparivitakkā aññatra diṭṭhinijjhānakkhantiyā ahametaṁ jānāmi ahametaṁ passāmi:
root_text.sn12.68:11.1: “Aññatreva, āvuso nārada, saddhāya aññatra ruciyā aññatra anussavā aññatra ākāraparivitakkā aññatra diṭṭhinijjhānakkhantiyā atthāyasmato nāradassa paccattameva ñāṇaṁ:
root_text.sn12.68:11.4: “Aññatreva, āvuso paviṭṭha, saddhāya aññatra ruciyā aññatra anussavā aññatra ākāraparivitakkā aññatra diṭṭhinijjhānakkhantiyā ahametaṁ jānāmi ahametaṁ passāmi:
root_text.sn12.68:12.1: “Aññatreva, āvuso nārada, saddhāya aññatra ruciyā aññatra anussavā aññatra ākāraparivitakkā aññatra diṭṭhinijjhānakkhantiyā atthāyasmato nāradassa paccattameva ñāṇaṁ:
root_text.sn12.68:12.3: “Aññatreva, āvuso paviṭṭha, saddhāya aññatra ruciyā aññatra anussavā aññatra ākāraparivitakkā aññatra diṭṭhinijjhānakkhantiyā ahametaṁ jānāmi ahametaṁ passāmi:
root_text.sn12.68:13.2: “‘Bhavanirodho nibbānan’ti kho me, āvuso, yathābhūtaṁ sammappaññāya sudiṭṭhaṁ, na camhi arahaṁ khīṇāsavo.
root_text.sn12.68:13.3: Seyyathāpi, āvuso, kantāramagge udapāno, tatra nevassa rajju na udakavārako.
root_text.sn12.68:13.4: Atha puriso āgaccheyya ghammābhitatto ghammapareto kilanto tasito pipāsito, so taṁ udapānaṁ olokeyya.
root_text.sn12.68:13.5: Tassa ‘udakan’ti hi kho ñāṇaṁ assa, na ca kāyena phusitvā vihareyya.
root_text.sn12.68:13.6: Evameva kho, āvuso, ‘bhavanirodho nibbānan’ti yathābhūtaṁ sammappaññāya sudiṭṭhaṁ, na camhi arahaṁ khīṇāsavo”ti.
root_text.sn12.68:14.1: Evaṁ vutte, āyasmā ānando āyasmantaṁ paviṭṭhaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn12.68:14.2: “evaṁvādī tvaṁ, āvuso paviṭṭha, āyasmantaṁ nāradaṁ kiṁ vadesī”ti?
root_text.sn12.68:14.3: “Evaṁvādāhaṁ, āvuso ānanda, āyasmantaṁ nāradaṁ na kiñci vadāmi aññatra kalyāṇā aññatra kusalā”ti.

translation_text.sn12.68:1.1: At one time the venerables Musīla, Saviṭṭha, Nārada, and Ānanda were staying near Kosambī in Ghosita’s monastery.
translation_text.sn12.68:1.2: Then Venerable Saviṭṭha said to Venerable Musīla:
translation_text.sn12.68:1.3: “Reverend Musīla, apart from faith, endorsement, oral transmission, reasoned train of thought, or acceptance of a view after deliberation, do you personally know that
translation_text.sn12.68:1.4: rebirth is a requirement for old age and death?”
translation_text.sn12.68:1.5: “Reverend Saviṭṭha, apart from faith, endorsement, oral transmission, reasoned train of thought, or acceptance of a view after deliberation, I know and see that
translation_text.sn12.68:1.6: rebirth is a requirement for old age and death.”
translation_text.sn12.68:2.1: “Reverend Musīla, apart from faith, endorsement, oral transmission, reasoned train of thought, or acceptance of a view after deliberateation, do you personally know that
translation_text.sn12.68:2.2: continued existence is a requirement for rebirth …
translation_text.sn12.68:2.3: grasping is a requirement for continued existence …
translation_text.sn12.68:2.4: craving is a requirement for grasping …
translation_text.sn12.68:2.5: feeling is a requirement for craving …
translation_text.sn12.68:2.6: contact is a requirement for feeling …
translation_text.sn12.68:2.7: the six sense fields are requirements for contact …
translation_text.sn12.68:2.8: name and form are requirements for the six sense fields …
translation_text.sn12.68:2.9: consciousness is a requirement for name and form …
translation_text.sn12.68:2.10: choices are a requirement for consciousness …
translation_text.sn12.68:2.11: ignorance is a requirement for choices?”
translation_text.sn12.68:2.12: “Reverend Saviṭṭha, apart from faith, endorsement, oral transmission, reasoned train of thought, or acceptance of a view after deliberation, I know and see that
translation_text.sn12.68:2.13: ignorance is a requirement for choices.”
translation_text.sn12.68:3.1: “Reverend Musīla, apart from faith, endorsement, oral transmission, reasoned train of thought, or acceptance of a view after deliberation, do you personally know that
translation_text.sn12.68:3.2: when rebirth ceases, old age and death cease?”
translation_text.sn12.68:3.3: “Reverend Saviṭṭha, apart from faith, endorsement, oral transmission, reasoned train of thought, or acceptance of a view after deliberation, I know and see that
translation_text.sn12.68:3.4: when rebirth ceases, old age and death cease.”
translation_text.sn12.68:4.1: “Reverend Musīla, apart from faith, endorsement, oral transmission, reasoned train of thought, or acceptance of a view after deliberation, do you personally know that
translation_text.sn12.68:4.2: when continued existence ceases, rebirth ceases …
translation_text.sn12.68:4.3: when grasping ceases, continued existence ceases …
translation_text.sn12.68:4.7: when the six sense fields cease, contact ceases …
translation_text.sn12.68:4.8: when name and form cease, the six sense fields cease …
translation_text.sn12.68:4.9: when consciousness ceases name and form cease …
translation_text.sn12.68:4.12: “Reverend Saviṭṭha, apart from faith, endorsement, oral transmission, reasoned train of thought, or acceptance of a view after deliberation, I know and see that
translation_text.sn12.68:5.1: “Reverend Musīla, apart from faith, endorsement, oral transmission, reasoned train of thought, or acceptance of a view after deliberation, do you personally know that
translation_text.sn12.68:5.2: the cessation of continued existence is extinguishment?”
translation_text.sn12.68:5.3: “Reverend Saviṭṭha, apart from faith, endorsement, oral transmission, reasoned train of thought, or acceptance of a view after deliberation, I know and see that
translation_text.sn12.68:5.4: the cessation of continued existence is extinguishment.”
translation_text.sn12.68:6.1: “Then Venerable Musīla is a perfected one, with defilements ended.”
translation_text.sn12.68:6.2: When he said this, Musīla kept silent.
translation_text.sn12.68:7.1: Then Venerable Nārada said to Venerable Saviṭṭha,
translation_text.sn12.68:7.2: “Reverend Saviṭṭha, please let me answer these questions.
translation_text.sn12.68:7.4: and I will answer them for you.”
translation_text.sn12.68:7.5: “By all means, Venerable Nārada, try these questions.
translation_text.sn12.68:7.7: and you can answer them for me.”
translation_text.sn12.68:8.1: (Saviṭṭha repeats exactly the same series of questions, and Nārada answers just as Musīla did.)
translation_text.sn12.68:12.1: “Reverend Nārada, apart from faith, endorsement, oral transmission, reasoned train of thought, or acceptance of a view after deliberation, do you personally know that
translation_text.sn12.68:12.2: the cessation of continued existence is extinguishment?”
translation_text.sn12.68:12.3: “Reverend Saviṭṭha, apart from faith, endorsement, oral transmission, reasoned train of thought, or acceptance of a view after deliberation, I know and see that
translation_text.sn12.68:12.4: the cessation of continued existence is extinguishment.”
translation_text.sn12.68:13.1: “Then Venerable Nārada is a perfected one, with defilements ended.”
translation_text.sn12.68:13.2: “I have truly seen clearly with right wisdom that the cessation of continued existence is extinguishment. Yet I am not a perfected one.
translation_text.sn12.68:13.3: Suppose there was a well on a desert road that had neither rope nor bucket.
translation_text.sn12.68:13.4: Then along comes a person struggling in the oppressive heat, weary, thirsty, and parched.
translation_text.sn12.68:13.5: They’d know that there was water, but they couldn’t physically touch it.
translation_text.sn12.68:13.6: In the same way, I have truly seen clearly with right wisdom that the cessation of continued existence is extinguishment. Yet I am not a perfected one.”
translation_text.sn12.68:14.1: When he said this, Venerable Ānanda said to Venerable Saviṭṭha,
translation_text.sn12.68:14.2: “Reverend Saviṭṭha, what do you have to say to Venerable Nārada when he speaks like this?”
translation_text.sn12.68:14.3: “Reverend Ānanda, I have nothing to say to Venerable Nārada when he speaks like this, except what is good and wholesome.”

variant_text.sn12.68:1.1: musilo → mūsīlo (bj); musīlo (pts1ed, pts2ed) | paviṭṭho → saviṭṭho (bj, pts1ed, pts2ed)
